channel.title: Home on Ritvik Teegavarapu
channel.description: Recent content in Home on Ritvik Teegavarapu

item.title: Bookshelf
item.description:
“What an astonishing thing a book is… Books break the shackles of time.” — Carl Sagan

Reading is an important skill for any academic, but mathematicians are often the subject of jokes regarding their literacy of regular books. As such, I would like to share what books I am currently reading in both the mathematical and casual world! Please feel free to send any recommendations my way.

Academic

Bakry, Gentil, Ledoux
Reading
Dembo, Zeitouni
Reading

Non-Academic

Dostoevsky
Reading

item.title: CV
item.description: My CV is available upon request via email. Instead, I hope you enjoy a picture of bunnies on campus (credit to Bunnies @ Brown)!

item.title: Mentoring
item.description:
“The delicate balance of mentoring someone is not creating them in your own image, but giving them the opportunity to create themselves.” — Steven Spielberg

While I have not had much mentoring experience, I hope to change that in graduate school. In principle, I am willing to advise undergraduate students (provided capacity) with sufficient mathematical maturity, and I am always happy to advise indirectly via email!

APMA Directed Reading Program

For Brown undergraduates, I am happy to announce that I will be serving as a mentor in the APMA DRP for both semesters in the AY 2026 - 2027. You may refer to the following topics and the linked syllabus, and if interested, please apply through the form on the DRP website.

item.title: Outreach
item.description:
“Mathematics knows no races or geographic boundaries; for mathematics, the cultural world is one country.” — David Hilbert

Introducing mathematics is

STACK

I have been fortunate enough to

item.title: Presentations
item.description:
If you are at any of these future conferences, please say hello and I would love to chat!

Upcoming

Introducing STACK to Caltech
International Meeting of the STACK Community 2026, University of Nairobi, Nairobi, Kenya (Jul. 27 – 31, 2026)
Synergies between Geometry, Probability, and Computation in High Dimensions
ICERM Hot Topics Workshop, Brown University, Providence, RI (Jul. 31 – Aug. 2, 2026)

Talks

Gibbs Principles for Markovian Discrete-Time Interacting Particle Systems on Regular Graphs
Southern California Conference for Undergraduate Research, CSU Channel Islands, Camarillo, CA (Nov. 2025)
Gibbs Principles for Markovian Discrete-Time Interacting Particle Systems on Regular Graphs
SURF Seminar Day, Caltech, Pasadena, CA (Oct. 2025)
Towards Convergence Guarantees for Particle Swarm Optimization
Southern California Conference for Undergraduate Research, CSU San Bernardino, San Bernardino, CA (Nov. 2024)
Towards Convergence Guarantees for Particle Swarm Optimization
SURF Seminar Day, Caltech, Pasadena, CA (Aug. 2024)
Incorporating Stochastic Loading into Droplet-Based Digital PCR Model
SURF Seminar Day, Caltech, Pasadena, CA (Oct. 2023)
Classification of Artificial & Real Objects Using Faster R-CNNs
IEEE Symposium Series on Computational Intelligence, Orange County Convention Center, Orlando, FL (Dec. 2021)

Posters

Towards Convergence Guarantees for Particle Swarm Optimization
Bayesian Inverse Problems and UQ Workshop, Brown University, Providence, RI (Mar. 2026)
Towards Convergence Guarantees for Particle Swarm Optimization — Best Poster Award
National Conference on Undergraduate Research, David L. Lawrence Convention Center, Pittsburgh, PA (Apr. 2025)
Towards Convergence Guarantees for Particle Swarm Optimization
Southern California Applied Mathematics Symposium (SOCAMS), UC Riverside, Riverside, CA (Apr. 2025)

item.title: Publications
item.description:
(*) denotes equal contribution.

Preprints

Towards Convergence Guarantees for Particle Swarm Optimization
Ritvik Teegavarapu, Dohyeon Kim, Franca Hoffmann
In preparation, 2026

Conference Papers

Classification of Artificial & Real Objects Using Faster R-CNNs
Ritvik Teegavarapu, Debojit Biswas
2021 IEEE Symposium Series on Computational Intelligence, Dec. 2021

Miscellaneous

Introducing STACK to Caltech
Jasmine Bryant, Jinghao Cao, Franca Hoffmann, Jodie Lee, Salvatore Mercuri, Ritvik Teegavarapu
Case Study, Mar. 2025
Competitive Calculus Problems
Ritvik Teegavarapu
Book, 2020

item.title: Research
item.description:
“I regard as quite useless the reading of large treatises of pure analysis: too large a number of methods pass at once before the eyes. It is in the works of applications that one must study them; one judges their ability there and one apprises the manner of making use of them.” — J.-L. Lagrange

My research interests lie at the interface of PDEs and probability, with a focus on interacting particle systems (IPS), where collective behavior emerges from local randomness in ways that reflect both the dynamics and the geometry of the underlying interactions.

item.title: Teaching
item.description:
“In a completely rational society, the best of us would be teachers and the rest of us would have to settle for something less, because passing civilization along from one generation to the next ought to be the highest honor and the highest responsibility anyone could have.” — Lee Iacocca

Teaching is an integral part of my academic identity, and one of my greatest passions that I have been able to pursue as an undergraduate student. My philosophy is that good teaching is like writing a good book, wherein you should know your audience, tell a compelling story, and leave something that lasts. By serving as a teaching assistant for a wide variety of courses, I have learned to slow down and meet students where they are, treat teaching as a genuine two-way intellectual exchange, and make abstract mathematics tangible for students encountering it for the first time. You can find my teaching statement and comments from the courses I have served as a TA for. My full teaching portfolio can be made available upon request.
